'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.

In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4, [[5th month|5th month]] 23rd day ([[July 5]], [[1847]]) | death_date = [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4, [[5th month|5th month]] 28th day ([[July 17]], [[1868]]) | childhood_name = Sui no Suke | later_names = Tadayuki | pseudonym = Aijo | posthumous_name = | dharma_name = Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi | resting_place = {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Kami]], [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-ge]], [[Jūsanmi|Posthumous Jūsanmi]] | bakufu = [[Edo bakufu|Edo Shogunate]] [[Temple and Shrine Magistrate|Jisha-bugyō]] and [[Sōshaban]] | master = [[Tokugawa Iesada]], [[Tokugawa Iemochi]] | domain = [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke]] [[Utsunomiya Domain]] | clan = {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} | father = {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} | siblings = {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}, {{ill|Toda [[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.

'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period and the lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]] in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]], and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of [[:en:Utsunomiya Domain|Utsunomiya Domain]].
In [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4 (1868), during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.

Although [[:en:Utsunomiya Castle|Utsunomiya Castle]] was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of [[:en:Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|Matsudaira Mitsunori]]
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu}} period and the lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}} in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}, and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}}.
In {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4 (1868), during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although {{ill|Utsunomiya Castle|en|Utsunomiya Castle|WD=Q8010544|lt=Utsunomiya Castle}} was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

date|1868|7|17}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Q108497391|WD=Q108497391}}|father={{ill|Toda Tadaharu|WD=Q10862122}}|given_name={{ill|Tadayuki|WD=Q16291783}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10862117]] Wikidata name: Toda Tadayuki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=2019th year 3月1日 (金) 14:13 (UTC)}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Toda Tadayuki | image = Toda Tadayuki(宇都宮藩主).jpg | imagesize = 200px | caption = Portrait of Toda Tadayuki | era = Late [[Edo period]] (Bakumatsu) | birth_date = {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4, [[5th month|5th month]] 23rd day ([[July 5]], [[1847]]) | death_date = {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4, [[5th month|5th month]] 28th day ([[July 17]], [[1868]]) | childhood_name = Sui no Suke | later_names = Tadayuki | pseudonym = Aijo | posthumous_name = | dharma_name = Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi | resting_place = {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]] | Rank = [[Jūgoi|Jūgoi-no-ge]], {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen no Kami}}, [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-ge]], [[Jūsanmi|Posthumous Jūsanmi]] | bakufu = [[Edo bakufu|Edo Shogunate]] [[Temple and Shrine Magistrate|Jisha-bugyō]] and [[Sōshaban]] | master = [[Tokugawa Iesada]], [[Tokugawa Iemochi]] | domain = [[Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke]] [[Utsunomiya Domain]] | clan = {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} | father = {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} | siblings = {{ill|Toda Tadamoto|1=Toda Tadaaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862118}}, Tadayuki | spouse = {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}'s daughter | children = Adopted: {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}} | notable_works = }}
[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu}} period and the lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}} in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}, and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}}.
In {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4 (1868), during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although {{ill|Utsunomiya Castle|en|Utsunomiya Castle|WD=Q8010544|lt=Utsunomiya Castle}} was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|author=Ryogo Shima|title=Shimotsuke Kaidō Monogatari|publisher=[[Shimotsuke Shimbunsha]]|year=1999|isbn=4-88286-105-4}} * ''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo'' (近藤出版社, 1975)

[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō}} of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu}} period and the lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}} in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}, and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain}}.
In {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4 (1868), during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although {{ill|Utsunomiya Castle|en|Utsunomiya Castle|WD=Q8010544|lt=Utsunomiya Castle}} was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori}}
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

[[File:Grave of TODA Tadayuki, Site of Eigan-ji temple.jpg|thumb|Grave of Toda Tadayuki]]
'''Toda Tadayuki''' (戸田 忠恕, also read as Tadakumi or Tadahiro) was a {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|WD=Q61982|lt=daimyō|1=Daimyo}} of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu|1=Bakumatsu}} period and the lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain|1=Utsunomiya Domain}} in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}. He was the 14th head of the main branch of the {{ill|Toda clan|ja|戸田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda clan|1=Toda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496536}} (Tahara branch) and the 12th head of the Toda family of the Utsunomiya Domain. Born as the sixth son of the domain lord {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}. His formal wife was a daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori|1=Matsudaira Mitsuhisa}}, and his adopted son was {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, his cousin of the same age.
== Life ==
Toda Tadayuki was born on the 23rd day of the 5th month of {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka}} 4 (July 5, 1847), as the sixth son of {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}}, lord of {{ill|Utsunomiya Domain|en|Utsunomiya Domain|WD=Q11449814|lt=Utsunomiya Domain|1=Utsunomiya Domain}}.
In {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3 (1856), he succeeded his brother {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}} after the latter’s death, receiving the court Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Jūgoi-no-ge]] and the title of {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|越前守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 越前国|1=Echizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1047144}}. As he was still young, his rule was su11orted by senior retainers such as {{ill|Mase Wazaburō|ja|間瀬和三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mase Wazaburō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸田忠至|1=Toda Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862132}} (later Toda Tadashi). During his rule, Tadayuki oversaw the repair of imperial mausoleums, an achievement for which he was promoted to the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Jūshi-i-no-ge]].<ref>[http://www.gokoku.gr.jp/museum_b01.html Shrine Deity Toda Tadayuki] Tochigi Prefectural Gokoku Shrine</ref>
In {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4 (1868), during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, he sided with the {{ill|Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja|新政府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Army (1868–1869)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官軍|1=kangun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10809959}}. Although {{ill|Utsunomiya Castle|en|Utsunomiya Castle|WD=Q8010544|lt=Utsunomiya Castle|1=Utsunomiya Castle}} was briefly taken by {{ill|pro-Tokugawa forces|ja|旧幕府軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pro-Tokugawa forces|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the castle was recaptured with imperial reinforcements. Tadayuki fell ill and died shortly after on the 28th day of the 5th month (July 17, 1868) at the age of 22. His grave is located at {{ill|Eiganji Temple|ja|英巌寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiganji Temple|1=Eigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109597112}} in Utsunomiya, and his posthumous dharma name is Reikōinden Jingaku Chūretsu Daikoshi.
In [[Meiji]] 2 (1869), he was posthumously awarded [[Jūshi-i|Jūshi-i-no-jō]], and in Meiji 30 (1897), he was promoted to [[Jūsanmi]].<ref>''Zōi Shokenden Zōhohanjo,'' p.8</ref> He is enshrined as a deity at the {{ill|Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|ja|栃木県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}.
== Family ==
'''Parents:''' * Father: {{ill|Toda Tadatomo|ja|戸田忠温|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadatomo|1=Toda Tadaharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862122}} * Foster Father: {{ill|Toda Tadaaki|ja|戸田忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadaaki|1=Toda Tadaaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862119}}
'''Spouse:''' * Shima-ko, daughter of {{ill|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|en|Matsudaira Mitsuhisa|WD=Q6832376|lt=Matsudaira Mitsunori|1=Matsudaira Mitsuhisa}}
'''Adopted Son:''' * {{ill|Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|ja|戸田忠友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadayuki (adopted son)|1=Toda Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10862109}}, eldest son of {{ill|Toda Tadashi|ja|戸田忠偲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toda Tadashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
